description: TimeSheet grid click handling distinguishes empty cells, single-entry cells, and multi-entry cells as separate interaction paths.
Clicking a multi-entry grid cell switches the screen into list view instead of opening the time-entry dialog directly.
TimeSheet owns a temporary focus-filter state describing the clicked work item, date, and entry ids for a multi-entry grid cell.
TimeSheetListView accepts the temporary focus filter and renders only the exact matching entries from the clicked grid cell.
Filtered list mode hides unrelated day groups and unrelated entry rows entirely instead of merely dimming or collapsing them.
A filtered-state banner appears above the list describing the active focus filter with work item name, date, and entry count.
The filtered-state banner includes a Clear filter action that removes the filter while keeping the user in list view.
The filtered-state banner includes a Back to grid action that clears the filter and returns the user to grid view.
Manual switching from list view back to grid view clears any active multi-entry focus filter.
Clicking a row from filtered list mode continues to open the existing editor flow for exactly one time entry.
TimeEntryDialog is simplified so it always edits exactly one entry and no longer accepts or renders a multi-entry editing mode.
TimeEntryDialog exposes only one clear Save action for the active entry and removes the competing multi-entry save model.
TimeEntryDialog removes its internal entry list and no longer allows users to jump between multiple entries inside the dialog.
TimeEntryDialog removes its in-dialog Add Entry affordance so new entries are initiated only from the grid or list surfaces.
Existing empty-cell create-entry behavior remains unchanged and still opens a single-entry creation flow.
Existing single-entry grid-cell behavior remains unchanged and still opens direct single-entry editing.
If the active filtered entry set becomes empty after a save or delete, the UI automatically clears the filter and leaves the user in a coherent destination state.
Delegated editing and normal timesheet editability rules continue to govern all grid, list, and dialog actions in the new flow.
